digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design standard and skill integration
propositions: Create a canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals with a scale-to-the-bug principle.
Each consuming skill (rebar-debug, rebar-implement, rebar-brainstorm) ships a real byte-identical copy of test-design.md in its own directory, using no symlinks and no user-/machine-specific paths.

A gating unit test tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py asserts byte-identity of every copy against the canonical file and self-verifies detection via a perturbed tmp_path copy, running under make test.

rebar-debug SKILL.md is updated: Step 4 retitled to RED-oracle design deferring LLM-surface evals to the shared standard, Step 5 gains defect-seeded mutation and a contract-card fourth handoff item, and the llm-behavioral-taxonomy Phase 2 bullet is qualified to cite test-design.md.

rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b and rebar-brainstorm's Before-recording-tickets section cite the shared file for oracle design, and README.md documents the byte-identical-copy convention and sync one-liner.
A style-check comment must be recorded on the ticket affirming positive-first phrasing and short prohibitions across all six edited prompt files, with make lint and make test passing.
